question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply q=circulating with a historical timestamp to getSupply() to obtain the releasedSupply() = TOTAL_SUPPLY - unreleasedSupply at that historical moment, and by comparing values across timestamps, determine the exact consensus timestamps when unreleased HBAR accounts were modified, leaking the history of treasury/escrow account balance changes in the Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative-equivalent timestamp (e.g., timestamp=gte:0.000000001) to getSupply() to cause Bound.of() to set lowerBound=1 nanosecond, and then NetworkServiceImpl.getSupply() to call getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1) with a near-zero maxTimestamp, causing ZoneOffset.UTC date arithmetic to underflow or produce an epoch-era date that results in an incorrect optimalLowerBound, returning supply data from the wrong historical period?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the convertToCurrencyFormat() method in NetworkSupplyMapper by triggering a supply query that returns a negative releasedSupply() (if unreleasedSupply > TOTAL_SUPPLY due to data corruption or manipulation), causing String.valueOf(negativeValue) to produce a string with a leading minus sign that is then incorrectly formatted by the substring logic, returning a malformed currency string that misrepresents the historical supply?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply file.id=0.0.101 to getNodes() to access the alternative address book (file 101 vs default 102), causing getAddressBookFileId() to return the ID of the secondary address book and exposing a different historical view of the Hashgraph node topology that may include nodes removed from the primary address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an arbitrary file.id value (e.g., file.id=0.0.1) to getNodes() to query the address_book table for any file ID, causing the findNetworkNodes() SQL query to return address book entries associated with non-standard file IDs that were historically stored in the Hashgraph, leaking historical node configurations that were never intended to be publicly queryable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() always returns the latest address book (latest start_consensus_timestamp DESC) regardless of the file.id parameter, and by supplying different file.id values and observing which returns nodes, enumerate all historical address book file IDs that have ever been stored in the Hashgraph mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple node.id range parameters (e.g., node.id=gte:0&node.id=lte:999999) to getNodes() to enumerate all node IDs in the latest address book, and then correlate the returned admin_key, public_key, and nodeCertHash fields with historical Hashgraph transactions to reconstruct the complete history of node key rotations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NETWORK_NODE_EXTRACTOR lambda that uses node.getNodeId().toString() to generate pagination next-link parameters, and by following the next link chain, paginate through all nodes in the address book to reconstruct the complete current Hashgraph node topology including service endpoints, IP addresses, and ports that could be used to target consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply node.id=eq:X combined with node.id=gte:Y to getNodes() to trigger the combined equality+range filter logic in NetworkServiceImpl.getNetworkNodes(), and by observing whether a node appears in the result, perform a timing-based oracle to determine whether a specific node ID exists in the current address book without enumerating all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit=1 with order=DESC to getNodes() to retrieve the highest node.id in the current address book, and then use binary search with node.id=gte:X queries to enumerate the exact set of active node IDs in the Hashgraph without triggering any rate limiting, enabling targeted DoS against specific consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() returns the grpcProxyEndpoint (domain_name, ip_address_v4, port) from the node table, which reflects the current Hashgraph node configuration, to obtain the gRPC proxy endpoints of all consensus nodes and use them to send crafted gRPC requests that attempt to manipulate the node's view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply file.id=0.0.102 with a very large node.id range (node.id=gte:0&node.id=lte:9223372036854775807) to getNodes() to cause the findNetworkNodes() SQL query to scan the entire address_book_entry table with maxNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing a full table scan that degrades database performance and prevents legitimate address book queries from reflecting the current Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply registered_node_id=gte:0 to getRegisteredNodes() to enumerate all registered nodes in the system, and by correlating the returned registeredNodeId values with the associatedRegisteredNodes field from getNodes(), reconstruct the complete mapping between consensus nodes and registered nodes in the current Hashgraph topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply type=BLOCK_NODE to getRegisteredNodes() to enumerate all block nodes registered in the Hashgraph, and use the returned endpoint information to target block nodes with crafted requests that attempt to inject false historical block data into the mirror node's view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the REGISTERED_NODE_EXTRACTOR lambda that uses node.getRegisteredNodeId().toString() for pagination, and by following the next-link chain with limit=1, enumerate all registered node IDs one by one to reconstruct the complete registered node history without triggering bulk-query rate limits?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply registered_node_id=eq:X combined with registered_node_id=gte:Y to getRegisteredNodes() to trigger the IllegalArgumentException('The eq operator cannot be combined with other operators') error path, and by observing the error vs success response, perform a boolean oracle to determine whether a specific registered node ID exists in the Hashgraph without enumerating all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TimestampParameter.valueOf() pattern (regex: ^((eq|gt|gte|lt|lte|ne):)?(\\d{1,17})(\\.(\\d{1,9}))?$) to supply a timestamp with 17-digit seconds (e.g., 99999999999999999) that causes DomainUtils.convertToNanos() to overflow a long, wrapping to a negative nanosecond value that is then used as a lower or upper bound in the file_data query, returning exchange rate data from an unintended historical position?
